task: Issue #2003: spec the Phase 1 agent-skill classification audit and execute TASK-011 to produce the audit deliverable. Address user constraint: too many agents and skills loaded, Claude Code is truncating the catalog at session start.

events.content: Invoke spec skill on issue #2003
Step 0.5 Memory-First Gate
Write 4 spec artifacts: PRD + REQ-011 + DESIGN-011 + TASK-011
Step 9 critic checks 9a/9b/9c/9d
Lock PRD §11 open questions before commit
Commit spec artifacts on feat/issue-2003-agent-skill-audit-spec
Execute TASK-011: mechanical scoring of c1, c5, has_shared_template for all 23 agents
Execute TASK-011: c3 (sibling-is-skill) for c1=Yes agents with <3 pipelines
Execute TASK-011: c4 (PR schema-drift history) for structured-output agents
Execute TASK-011: c2 (reference vs reasoning) heuristic + manual deflation
Execute TASK-011: apply verdict rule per DESIGN-011 §C3
Write audit document at .agents/audits/2026-05-10-agent-skill-classification-audit.md
File Phase 3 CI check stub issue #2008 (per PRD AC-12)
File validator bug #2007 (per user directive)
Round 2: address 13 PR review threads (4 bot reviewers: copilot, devin, cursor, coderabbit)
Round 3: address 3 follow-up review threads (roadmap c1 miss, em-dash in own correction, episode lessons empty)
Round 4: address code-review agent finding (1 P1)
Round 5: address 7 follow-up review threads (5 stale-numbers, 2 grep-method docs)
Round 6: address 7 follow-up review threads (1 count, 1 path quote, 1 path style, 2 false-positive semgrep, 2 grep portability)
Round 7: restore episode manual corrections + file extractor bug as #2013
Round 7d: harmonize critic c2 evidence format
Commit: e4ee4bd0

lessons: Copilot CLI sessions need to mark Serena MUSTs Complete=true with evidence describing the filesystem-equivalent memory access path; the validator only checks the boolean.

Pre-commit dash-prohibition enforcer (per .claude/rules/universal.md MUST NOT entry 5) flags both em-dash (U+2014) and en-dash (U+2013); replacement strategy: em-dash to comma, en-dash to hyphen, then clean up space-comma artifacts.
Spec-schemas.md enum drift is exactly the failure mode this audit addresses; reading the schema before writing REQ/DESIGN/TASK frontmatter is the defense.

spec-generator scores 4/4 on the discriminator (only agent to do so), validating the issue #2003 hypothesis. The 7 other skill candidates score 2-3/4, mostly via c1+c2+c3 (reference-heavy bodies invoked from slash commands with sibling skills).
Heuristic c2 scoring over-counts reasoning-focused agents because all-bullets are flagged as structured. Manual review needed for borderline cases per PRD §11 count rule.

11 of 23 agents have c1=No (12 of 23 agents are NOT invoked from any slash command was the round-2 number; round 3 moved roadmap from c1=No to c1=Yes via the descriptive Task() form in review.md, bringing the count back to 11; verified against the 23-row audit table: 12 agents show c1=1 and 11 show c1=0). These either represent peer-only invocations, dead code, or genuine but undiscoverable agents. Out of #2003 scope; recommend follow-up triage.
